 {"id":"bd-2qbx","title":"Contextual Help System","description":"## Overview
Provide integrated contextual help overlays that guide users through application interfaces.

## Rationale
- Terminal UIs lack discoverability compared to GUIs
- Users need help discovering keyboard shortcuts and features
- Tooltips and guided tours improve onboarding
- Reduces support burden through self-service

## Core Components
1. **HelpRegistry**: Central registry mapping widget IDs to help content
2. **Tooltip Widget**: Floating help text near focused elements
3. **Guided Tour**: Step-by-step walkthrough with highlights
4. **Keybinding Display**: Contextual shortcut hints
5. **Search Integration**: Fuzzy search across all help content

## Implementation Strategy
- Add HelpProvider trait for widgets to declare their help content
- Create HelpOverlay widget in ftui-extras
- Integrate with FocusManager for contextual awareness
- Store help content as structured data (not just strings)

## Help Content Structure
- Short description (tooltip)
- Long description (detail panel)
- Related keybindings
- See also links
- Examples

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for help registry and lookup
- Snapshot tests for tooltip rendering
- Integration tests for guided tour flow
- Accessibility tests for screen reader compatibility

## Success Criteria
- F1 or ? shows contextual help for focused widget
- Guided tours complete without user confusion
- Help content stays in sync with actual functionality

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-2qbx.1","title":"Help Registry Core","description":"Central registry for widget help content.

## Requirements
- Map widget IDs to help entries
- Hierarchical help (widget -> container -> app)
- Registration API for widgets
- Lazy loading of help content

## Implementation
- HelpRegistry struct
- HelpEntry with fields for all content types
- register_help(), get_help() methods

## Tests
- Registration and lookup
- Hierarchy resolution
- Missing help handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Help Registry Core
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2qbx.2","title":"Tooltip Widget","description":"Floating tooltip near focused widget.

## Requirements
- Position near focus bounds
- Auto-dismiss on focus change
- Configurable delay before show
- Multi-line content support

## Proposed API

```rust
/// Tooltip configuration
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct TooltipConfig {
    pub delay_ms: u64,           // Delay before showing (default: 500)
    pub max_width: u16,          // Max width before wrapping (default: 40)
    pub position: TooltipPosition,
    pub dismiss_on_key: bool,    // Dismiss on any keypress (default: true)
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum TooltipPosition {
    #[default]
    Auto,           // Automatically choose based on space
    Above,
    Below,
    Left,
    Right,
}

/// Tooltip widget (rendered as overlay)
pub struct Tooltip {
    content: String,
    config: TooltipConfig,
    target_bounds: Rect,
    visible: bool,
    show_timer: Option<Instant>,
}

impl Tooltip {
    pub fn new(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    pub fn config(self, config: TooltipConfig) -> Self;
    pub fn for_widget(self, bounds: Rect) -> Self;
    
    /// Calculate optimal position avoiding screen edges
    fn calculate_position(&self, screen: Rect) -> Position {
        let (w, h) = self.content_size();
        match self.config.position {
            TooltipPosition::Auto => {
                // Try below first, then above, then right, then left
                if self.target_bounds.bottom() + h <= screen.height {
                    Position::new(self.target_bounds.x, self.target_bounds.bottom())
                } else if self.target_bounds.y >= h {
                    Position::new(self.target_bounds.x, self.target_bounds.y - h)
                } else {
                    Position::new(self.target_bounds.right(), self.target_bounds.y)
                }
            }
            // ... other cases
        }
    }
}

/// Tooltip trigger wrapper
pub struct WithTooltip<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    tooltip: String,
    config: TooltipConfig,
}

impl<W: Widget> WithTooltip<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W, tooltip: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    pub fn delay(self, ms: u64) -> Self;
    pub fn position(self, pos: TooltipPosition) -> Self;
}

// Usage:
// Button::new("Save").with_tooltip("Save changes (Ctrl+S)")
```

## Focus Integration

Tooltip visibility controlled by FocusManager:
```rust
impl FocusManager {
    pub fn show_tooltip(&mut self, widget_id: WidgetId);
    pub fn hide_tooltip(&mut self);
    
    // Called on focus change
    fn on_focus_change(&mut self, old: WidgetId, new: WidgetId) {
        self.hide_tooltip();
        // Start tooltip timer for new focus
        self.tooltip_timer = Some(Instant::now());
    }
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-extras/src/help/tooltip.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_position_auto_below: Prefers below when space
- test_position_auto_above: Uses above when no space below
- test_position_screen_edge: Avoids going off-screen
- test_delay_timer: Shows after delay
- test_dismiss_on_blur: Hides on focus change
- test_multiline_wrap: Content wraps at max_width

## Snapshot Tests
- tooltip_below_80x24
- tooltip_above_80x24
- tooltip_multiline_80x24
- tooltip_at_edge_80x24

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tooltip placement never renders off-screen (tested across edge cases).
- [ ] Focus integration is correct: tooltip shows after delay and dismisses on focus change / keypress as configured.
- [ ] Multi-line wrapping is deterministic and snapshot-tested.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:20.805786824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:42:25.913236677Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:42:25.913208113Z","close_reason":"Implemented Tooltip widget with positioning, delay, and state management","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qbx.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1n5t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qbx.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qbx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qbx.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qbx.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qbx.6","title":"Contextual Help E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of contextual help system functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Tooltip Behavior (help_tooltip_e2e.rs)
- Tooltip appears on focus
- Correct positioning at edges
- Auto-dismiss on blur
- Multi-line content

### 2. Keybinding Hints (help_keybind_e2e.rs)
- Context-appropriate shortcuts
- Global shortcuts always visible
- Format correctness
- Update on focus change

### 3. Guided Tours (help_tour_e2e.rs)
- Tour step progression
- Target highlighting
- Navigation (next/back/skip)
- Completion tracking

### 4. Help Search (help_search_e2e.rs)
- Full-text search accuracy
- Fuzzy matching
- Result ranking
- Jump to widget

### 5. Accessibility (help_a11y_e2e.rs)
- Screen reader announcements
- Keyboard navigation
- Help panel focus management
- High contrast mode

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Help activations with widget context
- DEBUG: Search queries and results
- TRACE: Tour step transitions
- Metric: Help engagement rate

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/help_system/

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Contextual Help E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-bo6w","title":"Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences","description":"# Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences

## Background
Part of Terminal Capability Auto-Upgrade (bd-3227). Define capabilities that can be probed at runtime.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Capabilities that can be probed at runtime
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub enum ProbeableCapability {
    /// TrueColor (24-bit RGB) support
    TrueColor,
    /// Synchronized output (mode 2026)
    SynchronizedOutput,
    /// OSC 8 hyperlinks
    Hyperlinks,
    /// Kitty keyboard protocol
    KittyKeyboard,
    /// Sixel graphics
    Sixel,
    /// iTerm2 inline images
    ItermImages,
}

impl ProbeableCapability {
    /// All probeable capabilities
    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {
        &[
            Self::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages,
        ]
    }
    
    /// Get probe sequence bytes
    pub fn probe_sequence(&self) -> &'static [u8] {
        match self {
            // DA2 - Device Attributes Level 2
            Self::TrueColor => b"\x1b[>c",
            // Query mode 2026
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => b"\x1b[?2026$p",
            // Query XTGETTCAP for hyperlinks
            Self::Hyperlinks => b"\x1bP+q485950\x1b\\",
            // Query keyboard mode
            Self::KittyKeyboard => b"\x1b[?u",
            // Query Sixel support via DA1
            Self::Sixel => b"\x1b[c",
            // iTerm2 specific query
            Self::ItermImages => b"\x1b]1337;ReportCellSize\x07",
        }
    }
    
    /// Default timeout for this probe
    pub fn timeout(&self) -> Duration {
        match self {
            // Quick probes
            Self::TrueColor | Self::SynchronizedOutput => Duration::from_millis(100),
            // May need more time
            Self::Hyperlinks | Self::KittyKeyboard => Duration::from_millis(200),
            // Slower responses typical
            Self::Sixel | Self::ItermImages => Duration::from_millis(300),
        }
    }
    
    /// Convert to general Capability for upgrade
    pub fn to_capability(&self) -> Capability {
        match self {
            Self::TrueColor => Capability::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => Capability::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks => Capability::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard => Capability::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel => Capability::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages => Capability::Images,
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 probe capabilities defined
- [ ] Probe sequences are correct per terminal specs
- [ ] Timeouts are conservative but practical
- [ ] Mapping to general Capability works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:32.604035018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362989807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362925626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bo6w","depends_on_id":"bd-3227","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
